explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **proper nouns and formal titles within authoritative or structured contexts** (such as government officials' names paired with titles, legal/geographic terms like "grand larceny" or "Grand Canyon," and named entities in news/reporting).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across multiple languages (English, Italian, Russian) in news articles, official statements, and descriptive reports. It activates on topics like geopolitics (e.g., "Secretary of State Antony Blinken"), legal proceedings ("grand jury"), volcanic activity ("vulcanica"), and cultural figures ("Vladimir Nabokov"), often where specific named entities are embedded in formal or institutional phrasing.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: High-activation texts consistently contain **specific proper nouns combined with formal modifiers or roles** (e.g., "White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan," "Anak Krakatoa," "Grand Canyon chapter"), while low-activation examples lack such structured named entities—instead featuring generic topics (e.g., flu vaccination, data centers) or unnamed actors ("Federal Reserve Vice Chairman Richard Clarida" appears but without the surrounding formal context seen in high examples).

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects text segments where authoritative, institutional, or geographically-specific entities are explicitly identified, serving as a marker for structured information density in journalistic or descriptive prose.

results.actual_text: a near-constant in the latter stages of major tournaments. He won the World Grand Prix, Players Championship and Grand Slam of Darts in 2023
results.plain_text: Winning the Paddy Power PDC World Darts Championship has made Luke Humphries more aware of his privilege than ever.  And the world No1 is eager to put his platform to good use after beating Luke Littler to earn his maiden crown at Alexandra Palace this week. That victory has seen Humphries pulled in all directions, with even Prime Minister Rishi Sunak wanting a piece of the talent.  The leader of the Tory party was given a one-on-one session with Humphries, who made a trip to 10 Downing Street after his Ally Pally triumph and discussed his struggles with mental health on camera as part of the visit. And the world champ replied to one fan, who called darts star Humphries hypocritical for meeting with a Prime Minister who has overseen so many cuts that have "slashed mental health services and facilities."  "Did you ever think to yourself that maybe that was a massive reason for me to go?? We discussed that and how we can make sure everybody is getting the help they deserve," Humphries replied. "I have a massive platform to try and help others now and I will do that even talking to the PM to discuss it!"  The 28-year-old spoke to Sunak about his struggles with mental health and his battles against anxiety earlier in his career. And it's no coincidence that after developing a healthier relationship with that adversity, Humphries has seen a drastic improvement in his tournament results over the last few years.  Who do you think will win the next World Darts Championship? Let us know in the comments section below.  After reaching his first televised final at the 2021 UK Open, Humphries soon became a near-constant in the latter stages of major tournaments. He won the World Grand Prix, Players Championship and Grand Slam of Darts in 2023, capping off his year by winning his first World Championship.  'Cool Hand' continued on X (formerly Twitter): "I’m not a political person… I have no views on politics and I don’t support any constituency.. but I had the opportunity to meet rishi to talk mental health and darts. The PDC really wanted me to go and do that. And I’d have been silly not to use my platform to talk to him."  Join the Daily Star's WhatsApp for the sexiest headlines, showbiz gossip and lots more  The Daily Star is now on WhatsApp and we want you to join us!  Through the app, we'll send you the sassiest showbiz stories, some naught headline and a seismic smattering of aliens...along with the latest breaking news of course.  To join our community, all you have to do to join is click on this link, select 'Join Chat' and you're in!  No one will be able to see who has sign up and no one can send messages except for the Daily Star team. We also treat our community members to competitions, special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners.  If you don’t like our community, you can check out any time you like. To leave our community click on the name at the top of your screen and choose Exit group. If you’re curious, you can read our Privacy Notice.  The fan in question went on to praise Humphries for being so candid and open to discussion via social media, encouraging other celebrities and sports stars to do the same.  Regardless of where one falls on the political spectrum, the darts dynamo is correct that engaging with those in power is the only way to effect change. And after rising to his own position of power in the sports world, Humphries is eager to not allow his opportunity to go to waste.

The leader of the Tory party was given a one-on-one session with Humphries, who made a trip to 10 Downing Street after his Ally Pally triumph and discussed his struggles with mental health on camera as part of the visit.

results.actual_text: rează aproximativ 90% din activitatea seismică şi vulcanică a lumii şi unde se produc aproape 7.0
results.plain_text:
Un cutremur cu magnitudinea 6,2 s-a înregistrat duminică în largul coastelor Papua Noua Guinee, a anunţat Serviciului de prospectare geologică al Statelor Unite (USGS), fără să fie lansată alertă de tsunami, scrie Agerpres, citând AFP. Seismul produs la o adâncime de 38,2 kilometri. Cutremurul a avut epicentrul la 29 de kilometri est-nord-est de oraşul Kandrian, capitala provinciei West New Britain, potrivit USGS. Papua Noua Guinee se află în aşa-numitul "Cerc de Foc al Pacificului" Papua Noua Guinee se află în aşa-numitul "Cerc de Foc al Pacificului" 

 Papua Noua Guinee sau Noua Guinee este o țară insulară situată în vestul Oceanului Pacific (în Melanezia), la nord de Australia și la est de Indonezia. Împarte insula Noua Guinee cu provinciile Papua și Papua de Vest ale Indoneziei. Capitala este Port Moresby.  Relieful este predominant muntos, format din 2 lanțuri muntoase, de origine vulcanică, ale insulelor principale, și prelungirea acestora în insulițele vecine. Activitate vulcanică există în Arhipelagul Bismarck (Mt. Ulawun, 2.305 m) și în Insulele Bougainville (8985 km2).  Papua Noua Guinee se află în aşa-numitul "Cerc de Foc al Pacificului", o zonă unde se înregistrează aproximativ 90% din activitatea seismică şi vulcanică a lumii şi unde se produc aproape 7.000 de cutremure pe an, majoritatea de intensitate moderată.  Fiți la curent cu ultimele noutăți. Urmăriți DCNews și pe Google News

Seismul produs la o adâncime de 38,2 kilometri.

results.actual_text: Inc. Latest IPOs Videos Most Popular Mobile network software provider Mavenir lowers deal size by 8% ahead of $275 million
results.plain_text:
SPAC: Periphas Capital Partnering Corporation files for a $300 million IPO  Periphas Capital Partnering, a blank check company formed by Periphas Capital, filed on Thursday with the SEC to raise up to $300 million in an initial public offering. 
The New York, NY-based company plans to raise $300 million by offering 12 million units, which the company calls CAPS (Capital which Aligns and Partners with a Sponsor) securities, at a price of $25. Each CAPS security consists of one share of common stock and one-quarter of a warrant, exercisable at $28.75. At the proposed deal size, Periphas Capital Partnering would command a market value of $323 million and will receive a promote of only 6.25%, with additional "alignment shares" rewarded for outsized post-merger returns over a series of years. 
The company is led by CEO and Chairman Sanjeev Mehra, who co-founded Periphas Capital in 2017 and currently serves as Managing Partner, and CFO John Bowman, who was also a founding member of Periphas Capital and currently serves as CFO. Prior to Periphas, Mehra and Bowman both worked for over 20 years at Goldman Sachs. Periphas Capital Partnering plans to target technology-enabled businesses, business services, industrials, and consumer companies with enterprise values greater than $750 million.  
Periphas Capital Partnering Corporation was founded in 2020 and plans to list on the NYSE under the symbol PCPC.U. Evercore ISI is the sole bookrunner on the deal. 
 The article SPAC: Periphas Capital Partnering Corporation files for a $300 million IPO originally appeared on IPO investment manager Renaissance Capital's web site renaissancecapital.com. Investment Disclosure: The information and opinions expressed herein were prepared by Renaissance Capital's research analysts and do not constitute an offer to buy or sell any security. Renaissance Capital's Renaissance IPO ETF (symbol: IPO), Renaissance International ETF (symbol: IPOS), or separately managed institutional accounts may have investments in securities of companies mentioned.  The views and opinions expressed herein are the views and opinions of the author and do not necessarily reflect those of Nasdaq, Inc. results.actual_text: Federico Pereira, a detalles de ser nuevo jugador de Toluca para
results.plain_text: Federico Pereira, a detalles de ser nuevo jugador de Toluca para el Clausura 2024 Los Diablos Rojos del Toluca siguen reforzando su plantel de cara al Clausura 2024, pues la directiva del equipo fichó a Federico Pereira para así fortalecer la defensa del club para el próximo torneo del futbol mexicano.  Liverpool, equipo del futbol de Uruguay, confirmó en sus redes sociales la transferencia en donde agradecieron al lateral por su paso por el club "¡Tu casa y tu gente te esperan a la vuelta, Fede!".  Pereira tendrá así su primera experiencia por fuera del futbol uruguayo, pues ha pasado toda su carrera con el conjunto negriazul club donde levantó siete títulos al conseguir dos Torneo Intermedio, una Supercopa Uruguaya, un Campeonato Uruguayo, dos Torneo Clausura y un Torneo Apertura.  El lateral nacido en Montevideo, Uruguay, escribió en su cuenta de 'X' un mensaje de despedida del club, donde contó qué pasó más de diez años, agradeciendo a los hinchas y compañeros de equipo.  Federico se unió a los Diablos Rojos para conseguir el pase a Liguilla en el Clausura 2024, pues el equipo se quedó eliminado en el Apertura 2023 al no poderse clasificar.     Hoy toca despedirme de @LiverpoolFC1915 , fueron más de 10 años vistiendo esta camiseta. Agradecido a todos los hinchas, compañeros, funcionarios y todas las personas que hicieron posible q el club esté en el lugar q se merece!  Lo mejor en todo lo que se venga, fue un placer pic.twitter.com/APHXDZLR3M — Fedee (@fede_pereira6) December 30, 2023

results.actual_text: THE QUESTION Did The New York Times make Wordle harder? THE SOURCES
results.plain_text: THE QUESTION  Did The New York Times make Wordle harder?  THE SOURCES  THE ANSWER  No, The New York Times did not make Wordle harder.  WHAT WE FOUND  To understand whether the game’s gotten harder, you have to understand how the daily word is chosen.  This isn’t like the crossword, where puzzle masters decide the answers for each day. The answers were pre-determined long ago by the creator, Josh Wardle. There’s a set list of about 2,500 words that are assigned to different days.  So to see if the Times made the game harder, you can look at the source code for the original website’s word list, and compare it to the new one. (Yes, it’s possible to see the complete list, in order, so you could cheat if you really wanted. Spoiler alert: clicking those links will reveal the daily answers.)  When you download and compare the two lists, you’ll find they’re nearly identical. The only change is that the original list is six words longer.  When the Times took over, it removed six words (agora, pupal, lynch, fibre, slave, and wench) either because they might be offensive, or because they were just too obscure.  The Times did not add any new words. Any word that appears in this version of the game would have appeared in the original version too.  That means, if anything, the Times actually made the game easier.  A spokesperson for the Times confirmed this in a statement: "Since acquiring Wordle, we have not made the puzzle harder. We have not added any words to the solutions list, which was already predetermined by the game's original creator. On the contrary, we did remove a few obscure words (as an example, we removed 'AGORA'), in an effort to make the puzzle more accessible. We will continue to review the solutions, and remove obscure or potentially insensitive words."  The order of the words remains the same as the original site, too. However, since the Times removed those six words, some words were bumped up. That’s why people who still had access to the original website recently had “agora” as their answer, while most users had “aroma.” The Times did not add “aroma” to the word list; it was already next up after “agora," which was removed from the new site.

predictions.text: THE QUESTION Did The New York Times make Wordle harder? THE SOURCES THE ANSWER No, The New York Times did not make Wordle harder.
predictions.reasoning: Directly mentions The New York Times and Wordle, which are proper nouns in a journalistic context.

predictions.reasoning: It names Josh Wardle, the creator of Wordle, as a proper noun in an authoritative context.
